docstring.text: Attack executor module.

Adversarial configuration for attacks that involve adversarial chat targets.

This class defines the configuration for attacks that utilize an adversarial chat target,
including the target chat model, system prompt, and seed prompt for the attack.

signature.name: seed_prompt
signature.default: 'Generate your first message to achieve: {{ objective }}'

docstring.text:
Base class for all attack contexts.

This class holds both the immutable attack parameters and the mutable
execution state. The params field contains caller-provided inputs,
while other fields track execution progress.

Attacks that generate certain values internally (e.g., RolePlayAttack generates
next_message and prepended_conversation) can set the mutable override fields
(_next_message_override, _prepended_conversation_override) during _setup_async.

attributes.name: memory_labels
attributes.docstring: Additional labels that can be applied to the prompts throughout the attack.

attributes.name: next_message
attributes.docstring: Optional message to send to the objective target.

attributes.name: objective
attributes.docstring: Natural-language description of what the attack tries to achieve.

attributes.name: prepended_conversation
attributes.docstring: Conversation that is automatically prepended to the target model.

docstring.text:
Configuration for prompt converters used in attacks.

This class defines the converter configurations that transform prompts
during the attack process, both for requests and responses.

Manages the execution of attack strategies with support for parallel execution.

The AttackExecutor provides controlled execution of attack strategies with
concurrency limiting. It uses the attack's params_type to create parameters
from seed groups.

Execute attacks in parallel for each objective.

Creates AttackParameters directly from objectives and field values.

params.name: attack
params.desc: The attack strategy to execute.

params.name: objectives
params.desc: List of attack objectives.

params.name: field_overrides
params.desc:
Optional per-objective field overrides. If provided,
must match the length of objectives.

params.name: return_partial_on_failure
params.desc:
If True, returns partial results when some
objectives fail. If False (default), raises the first exception.

params.name: **broadcast_fields
params.desc:
Fields applied to all objectives (e.g., memory_labels).
Per-objective field_overrides take precedence.

returns.desc: AttackExecutorResult with completed results and any incomplete objectives.

raises.desc: If objectives is empty or field_overrides length doesn't match.
If return_partial_on_failure=False and any objective fails.

docstring.text:
Execute attacks in parallel, extracting parameters from SeedAttackGroups.

Uses the attack's params_type.from_seed_group() to extract parameters,
automatically handling which fields the attack accepts.

params.name: attack
params.desc: The attack strategy to execute.

params.name: seed_groups
params.desc: SeedAttackGroups containing objectives and optional prompts.

params.name: adversarial_chat
params.desc:
Optional chat target for generating adversarial prompts
or simulated conversations. Required when seed groups contain
SeedSimulatedConversation configurations.

params.name: objective_scorer
params.desc:
Optional scorer for evaluating simulated conversations.
Required when seed groups contain SeedSimulatedConversation configurations.

params.name: field_overrides
params.desc:
Optional per-seed-group field overrides. If provided,
must match the length of seed_groups. Each dict is passed to
from_seed_group() as overrides.

params.name: return_partial_on_failure
params.desc:
If True, returns partial results when some
objectives fail. If False (default), raises the first exception.

params.name: **broadcast_fields
params.desc:
Fields applied to all seed groups (e.g., memory_labels).
Per-seed-group field_overrides take precedence.

returns.desc: AttackExecutorResult with completed results and any incomplete objectives.

raises.desc: If seed_groups is empty or field_overrides length doesn't match.
If return_partial_on_failure=False and any objective fails.

docstring.text:
Execute the same attack strategy with multiple objectives against the same target in parallel.

.. deprecated::
    Use :meth:`execute_attack_async` instead. This method will be removed in a future version.

params.name: attack
params.desc: The attack strategy to use for all objectives.

params.name: objectives
params.desc: List of attack objectives to test.

params.name: prepended_conversation
params.desc: Conversation to prepend to the target model.

params.name: memory_labels
params.desc: Additional labels that can be applied to the prompts.

params.name: return_partial_on_failure
params.desc: If True, returns partial results on failure.

params.name: **attack_params
params.desc: Additional parameters specific to the attack strategy.

returns.desc: AttackExecutorResult with completed results and any incomplete objectives.

docstring.text:
Execute a batch of multi-turn attacks with multiple objectives.

.. deprecated::
    Use :meth:`execute_attack_async` instead. This method will be removed in a future version.

params.name: attack
params.desc: The multi-turn attack strategy to use.

params.name: objectives
params.desc: List of attack objectives to test.

params.name: messages
params.desc: List of messages to use for this execution (per-objective).

params.name: prepended_conversations
params.desc: Conversations to prepend to each objective (per-objective).

params.name: memory_labels
params.desc: Additional labels that can be applied to the prompts.

params.name: return_partial_on_failure
params.desc: If True, returns partial results on failure.

params.name: **attack_params
params.desc: Additional parameters specific to the attack strategy.

returns.desc: AttackExecutorResult with completed results and any incomplete objectives.

raises.desc: If the attack does not use MultiTurnAttackContext.

docstring.text:
Execute a batch of single-turn attacks with multiple objectives.

.. deprecated::
    Use :meth:`execute_attack_async` instead. This method will be removed in a future version.

params.name: attack
params.desc: The single-turn attack strategy to use.

params.name: objectives
params.desc: List of attack objectives to test.

params.name: messages
params.desc: List of messages to use for this execution (per-objective).

params.name: prepended_conversations
params.desc: Conversations to prepend to each objective (per-objective).

params.name: memory_labels
params.desc: Additional labels that can be applied to the prompts.

params.name: return_partial_on_failure
params.desc: If True, returns partial results on failure.

params.name: **attack_params
params.desc: Additional parameters specific to the attack strategy.

returns.desc: AttackExecutorResult with completed results and any incomplete objectives.

raises.desc: If the attack does not use SingleTurnAttackContext.

docstring.text: Initialize the attack executor with configurable concurrency control.

params.name: max_concurrency
params.desc: Maximum number of concurrent attack executions (default: 1).

raises.desc: If max_concurrency is not a positive integer.

docstring.text:
Result container for attack execution, supporting both full and partial completion.

This class holds results from parallel attack execution. It is iterable and
behaves like a list in the common case where all objectives complete successfully.

When some objectives don't complete (throw exceptions), access incomplete_objectives
to retrieve the failures, or use raise_if_incomplete() to raise the first exception.

Note: "completed" means the execution finished, not that the attack objective was achieved.
Get completed results, raising if any incomplete.

returns.desc: List of completed attack results.

docstring.text: Raise the first exception if any objectives are incomplete.

attributes.name: all_completed
attributes.docstring: Check if all objectives completed execution.

attributes.name: exceptions
attributes.docstring: Get all exceptions from incomplete objectives.

attributes.name: has_incomplete
attributes.docstring: Check if any objectives didn't complete execution.

attributes.name: input_indices
attributes.docstring:
Maps each completed result to its position in the original input sequence.

``input_indices[i]`` is the index in the original objectives/seed_groups/params
list that produced ``completed_results[i]``.  When some inputs fail, this lets
callers correlate results back to the specific input that produced them.

docstring.text:
Immutable parameters for attack execution.

This class defines the standard contract for attack parameters. All attacks
at a given level of the hierarchy share the same parameter signature.

Attacks that don't accept certain parameters should use the `excluding()` factory
to create a derived params type without those fields. Attacks that need additional
parameters should extend this class with new fields.

Create a new AttackParameters subclass that excludes the specified fields.

This factory method creates a frozen dataclass without the specified fields.
The resulting class inherits the `from_seed_group()` behavior and will raise
if excluded fields are passed as overrides.

params.name: *field_names
params.desc: Names of fields to exclude from the new params type.

returns.desc: A new AttackParameters subclass without the specified fields.

raises.desc: If any field_name is not a valid field of this class.

docstring.text:
Create an AttackParameters instance from a SeedAttackGroup.

Extracts standard fields from the seed group and applies any overrides.
If the seed_group has a simulated conversation config,
generates the simulated conversation using the provided adversarial_chat and scorer.

params.name: seed_group
params.desc: The seed attack group to extract parameters from.

params.name: adversarial_chat
params.desc:
The adversarial chat target for generating simulated conversations.
Required if seed_group has a simulated conversation config.

params.name: objective_scorer
params.desc:
The scorer for evaluating simulated conversations.
Required if seed_group has a simulated conversation config.

params.name: **overrides
params.desc: Field overrides to apply. Must be valid fields for this params type.

returns.desc: An instance of this AttackParameters type.

raises.desc: If seed_group has no objective or if overrides contain invalid fields.
If seed_group has simulated conversation but adversarial_chat/scorer not provided.

docstring.text:
Abstract base class for printing attack results.

This interface defines the contract for printing attack results in various formats.
Implementations can render results to console, logs, files, or other outputs.
Print only the conversation history.

params.name: result
params.desc: The attack result containing the conversation to print

params.name: include_scores
params.desc:
Whether to include scores in the output.
Defaults to False.

docstring.text: Print the complete attack result.

params.name: result
params.desc: The attack result to print

params.name: include_auxiliary_scores
params.desc:
Whether to include auxiliary scores in the output.
Defaults to False.

params.name: include_pruned_conversations
params.desc:
Whether to include pruned conversations.
For each pruned conversation, only the last message and its score are shown.
Defaults to False.

params.name: include_adversarial_conversation
params.desc:
Whether to include the adversarial
conversation (the red teaming LLM's reasoning). Only shown for successful
attacks to avoid overwhelming output. Defaults to False.

docstring.text: Print a summary of the attack result without the full conversation.

params.name: result
params.desc: The attack result to summarize

docstring.text:
Scoring configuration for evaluating attack effectiveness.

This class defines the scoring components used to evaluate attack effectiveness,
detect refusals, and perform auxiliary scoring operations.

Abstract base class for attack strategies.
Defines the interface for executing attacks and handling results.

Execute the attack strategy asynchronously with the provided parameters.

This method provides a stable contract for all attacks. The signature includes
all standard parameters (objective, next_message, prepended_conversation, memory_labels).
Attacks that don't accept certain parameters will raise ValueError if those
parameters are provided.

params.name: objective
params.desc: The objective of the attack.

params.name: next_message
params.desc: Message to send to the target.

params.name: prepended_conversation
params.desc: Conversation to prepend.

params.name: memory_labels
params.desc: Memory labels for the attack context.

params.name: **kwargs
params.desc: Additional context-specific parameters (conversation_id, system_prompt, etc.).

returns.desc: The result of the attack execution.

raises.desc: If required parameters are missing or if unsupported parameters are provided.

docstring.text: Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc: Optional[AttackScoringConfig]: The scoring configuration, or None if not applicable.

docstring.text: Get the objective target for this attack strategy.

returns.desc: The target system being attacked.

docstring.text: Get request converter configurations used by this strategy.

returns.desc: list[Any]: The list of request PromptConverterConfiguration objects.

attributes.name: params_type
attributes.docstring:
Get the parameters type for this attack strategy.

Returns:
    Type[AttackParameters]: The parameters type this strategy accepts.

docstring.text: Initialize the attack strategy with a specific context type and logger.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: context_type
params.desc: The type of context this strategy operates on.

params.name: params_type
params.desc:
The type of parameters this strategy accepts.
Defaults to AttackParameters. Use AttackParameters.excluding() to create
a params type that rejects certain fields.

params.name: logger
params.desc: Logger instance for logging events.

docstring.text:
Implementation of chunked extraction attack strategy.

This class orchestrates a multi-turn attack where protected information is
requested in chunks/segments across multiple turns. This technique bypasses
length-based filters or output truncation by asking for specific character
ranges of a secret value.

This technique was discovered during CTF red teaming exercises where targets
would reveal portions of secrets when asked for specific segments, even when
they refused to reveal the complete value.

The attack flow consists of:

1. Generating chunk request prompts based on the configured strategy.
2. Sending each chunk request to the target system sequentially.
3. Collecting responses from each chunk request.
4. Combining all chunk responses to reconstruct the full value.
5. Evaluating the combined result with scorers if configured.
6. Returning the attack result with achievement status.

The strategy supports customization through converters and scorers for
comprehensive evaluation.
Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc: Optional[AttackScoringConfig]: The scoring configuration with objective and auxiliary scorers.

docstring.text: Initialize the chunked request attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: chunk_size
params.desc: Size of each chunk to request (default: 50).

params.name: total_length
params.desc: Estimated total length of the target value (default: 200).

params.name: chunk_type
params.desc: Type of chunk to request (e.g., "characters", "bytes", "words").

params.name: request_template
params.desc:
Template for generating chunk requests
(default: "Give me {chunk_type} {start}-{end} of '{objective}'").

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for prompt converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring components.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for handling prompts.

raises.desc: If chunk_size or total_length are invalid.

docstring.text: Context for the ChunkedRequest attack strategy.

Console printer for attack results with enhanced formatting.

This printer formats attack results for console display with optional color coding,
proper indentation, text wrapping, and visual separators. Colors can be disabled
for consoles that don't support ANSI characters.

Print the conversation history to console with enhanced formatting.

Displays the full conversation between user and assistant, including:
- Turn numbers
- Role indicators (USER/ASSISTANT)
- Original and converted values when different
- Images if present
- Scores for each response

params.name: result
params.desc:
The attack result containing the conversation_id.
Must have a valid conversation_id attribute.

params.name: include_scores
params.desc:
Whether to include scores in the output.
Defaults to False.

params.name: include_reasoning_trace
params.desc:
Whether to include model reasoning trace in the output
for applicable models. Defaults to False.

docstring.text:
Print a list of messages to console with enhanced formatting.

This method can be called directly with a list of Message objects,
without needing an AttackResult. Useful for printing prepended_conversation
or any other list of messages.

Displays:
- Turn numbers
- Role indicators (USER/ASSISTANT/SYSTEM)
- Original and converted values when different
- Images if present
- Scores for each response (if include_scores=True)

params.name: messages
params.desc: List of Message objects to print.

params.name: include_scores
params.desc:
Whether to include scores in the output.
Defaults to False.

params.name: include_reasoning_trace
params.desc:
Whether to include model reasoning trace in the output
for applicable models. Defaults to False.

docstring.text:
Print the complete attack result to console.

This method orchestrates the printing of all components of an attack result,
including header, summary, conversation history, metadata, and footer.

params.name: result
params.desc: The attack result to print. Must not be None.

params.name: include_auxiliary_scores
params.desc:
Whether to include auxiliary scores in the output.
Defaults to False.

params.name: include_pruned_conversations
params.desc:
Whether to include pruned conversations.
For each pruned conversation, only the last message and its score are shown.
Defaults to False.

params.name: include_adversarial_conversation
params.desc:
Whether to include the adversarial
conversation (the red teaming LLM's reasoning). Only shown for successful
attacks to avoid overwhelming output. Defaults to False.

docstring.text:
Print a summary of the attack result with enhanced formatting.

Displays:
- Basic information (objective, attack type, conversation ID)
- Execution metrics (turns executed, execution time)
- Outcome information (status, reason)
- Final score if available

params.name: result
params.desc:
The attack result to summarize. Must contain
objective, attack_identifier, conversation_id, executed_turns,
execution_time_ms, outcome, and optionally outcome_reason and
last_score attributes.

docstring.text: Initialize the console printer.

params.name: width
params.desc:
Maximum width for text wrapping. Must be positive.
Defaults to 100.

params.name: indent_size
params.desc:
Number of spaces for indentation. Must be non-negative.
Defaults to 2.

params.name: enable_colors
params.desc:
Whether to enable ANSI color output. When False,
all output will be plain text without colors. Defaults to True.

raises.desc: If width <= 0 or indent_size < 0.

docstring.text:
Implementation of the context compliance attack strategy.

This attack attempts to bypass safety measures by rephrasing the objective into a more benign context.
It uses an adversarial chat target to:
1. Rephrase the objective as a more benign question
2. Generate a response to the benign question
3. Rephrase the original objective as a follow-up question

This creates a context that makes it harder for the target to detect the true intent.
Initialize the context compliance attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack. Must be a PromptChatTarget.

params.name: attack_adversarial_config
params.desc:
Configuration for the adversarial component,
including the adversarial chat target used for rephrasing.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc:
Configuration for attack converters,
including request and response converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for attack scoring.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: The prompt normalizer to use for sending prompts.

params.name: max_attempts_on_failure
params.desc: Maximum number of attempts to retry on failure.

params.name: context_description_instructions_path
params.desc:
Path to the context description
instructions YAML file. If not provided, uses the default path.

params.name: affirmative_response
params.desc:
The affirmative response to be used in the conversation history.
If not provided, uses the default "yes.".

raises.desc: If the context description instructions file is invalid.

docstring.text:
Manages conversations for attacks, handling message history,
system prompts, and conversation state.

This class provides methods to:
- Initialize attack context with prepended conversations
- Retrieve conversation history
- Set system prompts for chat targets

Add prepended conversation messages to memory for a chat target.

This is a lower-level method that handles adding messages to memory without
modifying any attack context state. It can be called directly by attacks
that manage their own state (like TAP nodes) or internally by
initialize_context_async for standard attacks.

Messages are added with:
- Duplicated message objects (preserves originals)
- simulated_assistant role for assistant messages (for traceability)
- Converters applied based on config

params.name: prepended_conversation
params.desc: Messages to add to memory.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: Conversation ID to assign to all messages.

params.name: request_converters
params.desc: Optional converters to apply to messages.

params.name: prepended_conversation_config
params.desc: Optional configuration for converter roles.

params.name: max_turns
params.desc: If provided, validates that turn count doesn't exceed this limit.

returns.desc: The number of turns (assistant messages) added.

raises.desc: If max_turns is exceeded by the prepended conversation.

docstring.text: Retrieve a conversation by its ID.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The ID of the conversation to retrieve.

returns.desc: A list of messages in the conversation, ordered by creation time.
Returns empty list if no messages exist.

docstring.text: Retrieve the most recent message from a conversation.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The ID of the conversation to retrieve from.

params.name: role
params.desc: If provided, return only the last message matching this role.

returns.desc: The last message piece, or None if no messages exist.

docstring.text:
Initialize attack context with prepended conversation and merged labels.

This is the primary method for setting up an attack context. It:
1. Merges memory_labels from attack strategy with context labels
2. Processes prepended_conversation based on target type and config
3. Updates context.executed_turns for multi-turn attacks
4. Sets context.next_message if there's an unanswered user message

params.name: context
params.desc: The attack context to initialize.

params.name: target
params.desc: The objective target for the conversation.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: Unique identifier for the conversation.

params.name: request_converters
params.desc: Converters to apply to messages.

params.name: prepended_conversation_config
params.desc: Configuration for handling prepended conversation.

params.name: max_turns
params.desc: Maximum turns allowed (for validation and state tracking).

params.name: memory_labels
params.desc: Labels from the attack strategy to merge with context labels.

returns.desc: ConversationState with turn_count and last_assistant_message_scores.

raises.desc:
If conversation_id is empty, or if prepended_conversation
requires a PromptChatTarget but target is not one.

docstring.text: Set or update the system prompt for a conversation.

params.name: target
params.desc: The chat target to set the system prompt on.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: Unique identifier for the conversation.

params.name: system_prompt
params.desc: The system prompt text.

params.name: labels
params.desc: Optional labels to associate with the system prompt.

docstring.text: Initialize the conversation manager.

params.name: attack_identifier
params.desc: The identifier of the attack this manager belongs to.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc:
Optional prompt normalizer for converting prompts.
If not provided, a default PromptNormalizer instance will be created.

docstring.text: Session for conversations.
Container for conversation state data returned from context initialization.

Implementation of the Crescendo attack strategy.

The Crescendo Attack is a multi-turn strategy that progressively guides the model to generate harmful
content through small, benign steps. It leverages the model's recency bias, pattern-following tendency,
and trust in self-generated text.

The attack flow consists of:
1. Generating progressively harmful prompts using an adversarial chat model.
2. Sending prompts to the target and evaluating responses for refusal.
3. Backtracking when the target refuses to respond.
4. Scoring responses to determine if the objective has been achieved.
5. Continuing until the objective is met or maximum turns/backtracks are reached.

You can learn more about the Crescendo attack at:
https://crescendo-the-multiturn-jailbreak.github.io/
Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc:
Optional[AttackScoringConfig]: The scoring configuration with objective scorer,
auxiliary scorers, and refusal scorer.

docstring.text: Initialize the Crescendo attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack. Must be a PromptChatTarget.

params.name: attack_adversarial_config
params.desc:
Configuration for the adversarial component,
including the adversarial chat target and optional system prompt path.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc:
Configuration for attack converters,
including request and response converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring responses.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for prompts.

params.name: max_backtracks
params.desc: Maximum number of backtracks allowed.

params.name: max_turns
params.desc: Maximum number of turns allowed.

params.name: prepended_conversation_config
params.desc:
Configuration for how to process prepended conversations. Controls converter
application by role, message normalization, and non-chat target behavior.

raises.desc: If objective_target is not a PromptChatTarget.

docstring.text: Context for the Crescendo attack strategy.
Result of the Crescendo attack strategy execution.

attributes.name: backtrack_count
attributes.docstring:
Get the number of backtracks performed during the attack.

Returns:
    int: The number of backtracks.

docstring.text:
Implement the FlipAttack method found here:
https://arxiv.org/html/2410.02832v1.

Essentially, it adds a system prompt to the beginning of the conversation to flip each word in the prompt.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for the prompt converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring components.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for handling prompts.

params.name: max_attempts_on_failure
params.desc: Maximum number of attempts to retry on failure.

docstring.text:
Implement the Many Shot Jailbreak method as discussed in research found here:
https://www.anthropic.com/research/many-shot-jailbreaking.

Prepends the seed prompt with a faux dialogue between a human and an AI, using examples from a dataset
to demonstrate successful jailbreaking attempts. This method leverages the model's ability to learn from
examples to bypass safety measures.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for the prompt converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring components.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for handling prompts.

params.name: max_attempts_on_failure
params.desc: Maximum number of attempts to retry on failure. Defaults to 0.

params.name: example_count
params.desc:
The number of examples to include from many_shot_examples or the Many
Shot Jailbreaking dataset. Defaults to the first 100.

params.name: many_shot_examples
params.desc:
The many shot jailbreaking examples to use.
If not provided, takes the first `example_count` examples from Many Shot Jailbreaking dataset.

raises.desc: If many_shot_examples is empty.

docstring.text:
Markdown printer for attack results optimized for Jupyter notebooks.

This printer formats attack results as markdown, making them ideal for display
in Jupyter notebooks where LLM responses often contain code blocks and other
markdown formatting that should be properly rendered.

Print only the conversation history as formatted markdown.

Extracts and displays the conversation messages from the attack result
without the summary or metadata sections. Useful for focusing on the
actual interaction flow.

The attack result containing the conversation
to display.

params.name: include_scores
params.desc:
Whether to include scores
for each message. Defaults to False.

docstring.text:
Print the complete attack result as formatted markdown.

Generates a comprehensive markdown report including attack summary,
conversation history, scores, and metadata. The output is optimized
for display in Jupyter notebooks.

params.name: result
params.desc: The attack result to print.

params.name: include_auxiliary_scores
params.desc:
Whether to include auxiliary scores
in the conversation display. Defaults to False.

params.name: include_pruned_conversations
params.desc:
Whether to include pruned conversations.
For each pruned conversation, only the last message and its score are shown.
Defaults to False.

params.name: include_adversarial_conversation
params.desc:
Whether to include the adversarial
conversation (the red teaming LLM's reasoning). Only shown for successful
attacks to avoid overwhelming output. Defaults to False.

docstring.text:
Print a summary of the attack result as formatted markdown.

Displays key information about the attack including objective, outcome,
execution metrics, and final score without the full conversation history.
Useful for getting a quick overview of the attack results.

params.name: result
params.desc: The attack result to summarize.

docstring.text: Initialize the markdown printer.

params.name: display_inline
params.desc:
If True, uses IPython.display to render markdown
inline in Jupyter notebooks. If False, prints markdown strings.
Defaults to True.

docstring.text:
Implementation of multi-prompt sending attack strategy.

This class orchestrates a multi-turn attack where a series of predefined malicious
prompts are sent sequentially to try to achieve a specific objective against a target
system. The strategy evaluates the final target response using optional scorers to
determine if the objective has been met.

The attack flow consists of:
1. Sending each predefined prompt to the target system in sequence.
2. Continuing until all predefined prompts are sent.
3. Evaluating the final response with scorers if configured.
4. Returning the attack result with achievement status.

Note: This attack always runs all predefined prompts regardless of whether the
objective is achieved early in the sequence.

The strategy supports customization through prepended conversations, converters,
and multiple scorer types for comprehensive evaluation.
Execute the attack strategy asynchronously with the provided parameters.

returns.desc: The result of the attack execution.

docstring.text: Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc: Optional[AttackScoringConfig]: The scoring configuration with objective and auxiliary scorers.

docstring.text: Initialize the multi-prompt sending attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for prompt converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring components.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for handling prompts.

raises.desc: If the objective scorer is not a true/false scorer.

docstring.text:
Parameters for MultiPromptSendingAttack.

Extends AttackParameters to include user_messages field for multi-turn attacks.
Only accepts objective and user_messages fields.
Create parameters from a SeedGroup, extracting user messages.

params.name: seed_group
params.desc: The seed group to extract parameters from.

params.desc: Not used by this attack type.

params.name: objective_scorer
params.desc: Not used by this attack type.

params.name: **overrides
params.desc: Field overrides to apply.

returns.desc: MultiPromptSendingAttackParameters instance.

raises.desc: If seed_group has no objective, no user messages, or if overrides contain invalid fields.

docstring.text:
Context for multi-turn attacks.

Holds execution state for multi-turn attacks. The immutable attack parameters
(objective, next_message, prepended_conversation, memory_labels) are stored in
the params field inherited from AttackContext.

Strategy for executing multi-turn attacks.
This strategy is designed to handle attacks that consist of multiple turns
of interaction with the target model.
Implement the base class for multi-turn attack strategies.

params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.desc: The type of context this strategy will use.

docstring.text:
Configuration for controlling how prepended conversations are processed before
being sent to the objective target.

This class provides control over:
- Which message roles should have request converters applied
- How to normalize conversation history for non-chat objective targets
- What to do when the objective target is not a PromptChatTarget
Create a default configuration with converters applied to all roles.

returns.desc: A configuration that applies converters to all prepended messages,
raising an error for non-chat targets.

docstring.text:
Create a configuration for use with non-chat targets.

This configuration normalizes the prepended conversation into a text block
that will be prepended to the first message sent to the target.

params.name: message_normalizer
params.desc:
Normalizer for formatting the prepended conversation into a string.
Defaults to ConversationContextNormalizer if not provided.

params.name: apply_converters_to_roles
params.desc:
Roles to apply converters to before normalization.
Defaults to all roles.

returns.desc: A configuration that normalizes the prepended conversation for non-chat targets.

docstring.text: Get the normalizer for objective target context, with a default fallback.

returns.desc: The configured objective_target_context_normalizer, or a default
ConversationContextNormalizer if none was configured.

docstring.text:
Implementation of single-turn prompt sending attack strategy.

This class orchestrates a single-turn attack where malicious prompts are injected
to try to achieve a specific objective against a target system. The strategy evaluates
the target response using optional scorers to determine if the objective has been met.

The attack flow consists of:
1. Preparing the prompt based on the objective.
2. Sending the prompt to the target system through optional converters.
3. Evaluating the response with scorers if configured.
4. Retrying on failure up to the configured number of retries.
5. Returning the attack result with achievement status.

The strategy supports customization through prepended conversations, converters,
and multiple scorer types for comprehensive evaluation.
Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc: Optional[AttackScoringConfig]: The scoring configuration with objective and auxiliary scorers.

docstring.text: Initialize the prompt injection attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for prompt converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring components.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for handling prompts.

params.name: max_attempts_on_failure
params.desc: Maximum number of attempts to retry on failure.

params.name: params_type
params.desc:
The type of parameters this strategy accepts.
Defaults to AttackParameters. Use AttackParameters.excluding() to create
a params type that rejects certain fields.

params.name: prepended_conversation_config
params.desc:
Configuration for how to process prepended conversations. Controls converter
application by role, message normalization, and non-chat target behavior.

raises.desc: If the objective scorer is not a true/false scorer.

docstring.text: Enum for predefined red teaming attack system prompt paths.

Implementation of multi-turn red teaming attack strategy.

This class orchestrates an iterative attack process where an adversarial chat model generates
prompts to send to a target system, attempting to achieve a specified objective. The strategy
evaluates each target response using a scorer to determine if the objective has been met.

The attack flow consists of:
1. Generating adversarial prompts based on previous responses and scoring feedback.
2. Sending prompts to the target system through optional converters.
3. Scoring target responses to assess objective achievement.
4. Using scoring feedback to guide subsequent prompt generation.
5. Continuing until the objective is achieved or maximum turns are reached.

The strategy supports customization through system prompts, seed prompts, and prompt converters,
allowing for various attack techniques and scenarios.
Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc:
Optional[AttackScoringConfig]: The scoring configuration with objective scorer
and use_score_as_feedback.

docstring.text: Initialize the red teaming attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: attack_adversarial_config
params.desc: Configuration for the adversarial component.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for attack converters. Defaults to None.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for attack scoring. Defaults to None.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: The prompt normalizer to use for sending prompts. Defaults to None.

params.name: max_turns
params.desc: Maximum number of turns for the attack. Defaults to 10.

params.name: score_last_turn_only
params.desc:
If True, only score the final turn instead of every turn.
This reduces LLM calls when intermediate scores are not needed (e.g., for
generating simulated conversations). The attack will run for exactly max_turns
when this is enabled. Defaults to False.

raises.desc: If objective_scorer is not provided in attack_scoring_config.

docstring.text:
Implementation of single-turn role-play attack strategy.

This class orchestrates a role-play attack where malicious objectives are rephrased
into role-playing contexts to make them appear more benign and bypass content filters.
The strategy uses an adversarial chat target to transform the objective into a role-play
scenario before sending it to the target system.

The attack flow consists of:
1. Loading role-play scenarios from a YAML file.
2. Using an adversarial chat target to rephrase the objective into the role-play context.
3. Sending the rephrased objective to the target system.
4. Evaluating the response with scorers if configured.
5. Retrying on failure up to the configured number of retries.
6. Returning the attack result

The strategy supports customization through prepended conversations, converters,
and multiple scorer types.
Initialize the role-play attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: adversarial_chat
params.desc:
The adversarial chat target used to rephrase
objectives into role-play scenarios.

params.name: role_play_definition_path
params.desc:
Path to the YAML file containing role-play
definitions (rephrase instructions, user start turn, assistant start turn).

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for prompt converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring components.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for handling prompts.

params.name: max_attempts_on_failure
params.desc: Maximum number of attempts to retry the attack

raises.desc: If the objective scorer is not a true/false scorer.
If the role_play_definition_path does not exist.

docstring.text: Enum for predefined role-play scenario paths.

Context for single-turn attacks.

Holds execution state for single-turn attacks. The immutable attack parameters
(objective, next_message, prepended_conversation, memory_labels) are stored in
the params field inherited from AttackContext.

Strategy for executing single-turn attacks.
This strategy is designed to handle attacks that consist of a single turn
of interaction with the target model.
Define a base class for single-turn attack strategies.

params.name: params_type
params.desc: The type of parameters this strategy accepts.

params.name: logger
params.desc: Logger instance for logging events and messages.

docstring.text:
Implementation of the skeleton key jailbreak attack strategy.

This attack sends an initial skeleton key prompt to the target, and then follows
up with a separate attack prompt. If successful, the first prompt makes the target
comply even with malicious follow-up prompts.

The attack flow consists of:
1. Sending a skeleton key prompt to bypass the target's safety mechanisms.
2. Sending the actual objective prompt to the primed target.
3. Evaluating the response using configured scorers to determine success.

Learn more about attack at the link below:
https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2024/06/26/mitigating-skeleton-key-a-new-type-of-generative-ai-jailbreak-technique/
Initialize the skeleton key attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for prompt converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring components.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for handling prompts.

params.name: skeleton_key_prompt
params.desc:
The skeleton key prompt to use.
If not provided, uses the default skeleton key prompt.

params.name: max_attempts_on_failure
params.desc: Maximum number of attempts to retry on failure.

docstring.text:
Context for the Tree of Attacks with Pruning (TAP) attack strategy.

This context contains all execution-specific state for a TAP attack instance,
ensuring thread safety by isolating state per execution.

Result of the Tree of Attacks with Pruning (TAP) attack strategy execution.

This result includes the standard attack result information with
attack-specific data stored in the metadata dictionary.

attributes.name: auxiliary_scores_summary
attributes.docstring: Get a summary of auxiliary scores from the best node.

attributes.name: best_adversarial_conversation_id
attributes.docstring: Get the adversarial conversation ID for the best-scoring branch.

attributes.name: max_depth_reached
attributes.docstring: Get the maximum depth reached in the attack tree.

attributes.name: nodes_explored
attributes.docstring: Get the total number of nodes explored during the attack.

attributes.name: nodes_pruned
attributes.docstring: Get the number of nodes pruned during the attack.

attributes.name: tree_visualization
attributes.docstring: Get the tree visualization from metadata.

docstring.text:
Implement the Tree of Attacks with Pruning (TAP) attack strategy.

The TAP attack strategy systematically explores multiple adversarial prompt paths in parallel
using a tree structure. It employs breadth-first search with pruning to efficiently find
effective jailbreaks while managing computational resources.

How it works:
1. **Initialization**: Creates multiple initial attack branches (width) to explore different approaches
2. **Tree Expansion**: For each iteration (depth), branches are expanded by a branching factor
3. **Prompt Generation**: Each node generates adversarial prompts via an LLM red-teaming assistant
4. **Evaluation**: Responses are evaluated for objective achievement and on-topic relevance
5. **Pruning**: Low-scoring or off-topic branches are pruned to maintain the width constraint
6. **Iteration**: The process continues until the objective is achieved or max depth is reached

The strategy balances exploration (trying diverse approaches) with exploitation (focusing on
promising paths) through its pruning mechanism.

returns.desc: The result of the TAP attack execution.

docstring.text: Execute the multi-turn attack strategy asynchronously with the provided parameters.

params.name: objective
params.desc: The objective of the attack.

params.name: memory_labels
params.desc: Memory labels for the attack context.

params.name: **kwargs
params.desc: Additional parameters for the attack.

returns.desc: The result of the attack execution.

docstring.text: Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc: The TAP-specific scoring configuration.

docstring.text: Initialize the Tree of Attacks with Pruning attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: attack_adversarial_config
params.desc: Configuration for the adversarial chat component.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc:
Configuration for attack converters.
Defaults to None.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc:
Scoring configuration for TAP.
The objective_scorer must be a FloatScaleThresholdScorer, which provides both
granular float scores for node comparison and a threshold for determining success.
Can be either AttackScoringConfig or TAPAttackScoringConfig. If not provided,
a default configuration with SelfAskScaleScorer and threshold 0.7 is created.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: The prompt normalizer to use. Defaults to None.

params.name: tree_width
params.desc: Number of branches to explore in parallel at each level. Defaults to 3.

params.name: tree_depth
params.desc: Maximum number of iterations to perform. Defaults to 5.

params.name: branching_factor
params.desc: Number of child branches to create from each parent. Defaults to 2.

params.name: on_topic_checking_enabled
params.desc: Whether to check if prompts are on-topic. Defaults to True.

params.name: desired_response_prefix
params.desc: Expected prefix for successful responses. Defaults to "Sure, here is".

params.name: batch_size
params.desc: Number of nodes to process in parallel per batch. Defaults to 10.

params.name: prepended_conversation_config
params.desc:
Configuration for how to process prepended conversations. Controls converter
application by role, message normalization, and non-chat target behavior.

raises.desc:
If attack_scoring_config uses a non-FloatScaleThresholdScorer objective scorer,
if target is not PromptChatTarget, or if parameters are invalid.

docstring.text:
Generate a simulated conversation between an adversarial chat and a target.

This utility runs a RedTeamingAttack with `score_last_turn_only=True` against a simulated
target (the same LLM as adversarial_chat, optionally configured with a system prompt).
The resulting conversation is returned as a list of SeedPrompts that can be merged with
other SeedPrompts in a SeedGroup for use as `prepended_conversation` and `next_message`.

Use cases:
- Creating role-play scenarios dynamically (e.g., movie script, video game)
- Establishing conversational context before attacking a real target
- Generating multi-turn jailbreak setups without hardcoded responses

params.name: objective
params.desc: The objective for the adversarial chat to work toward.

params.name: adversarial_chat
params.desc:
The adversarial LLM that generates attack prompts.
This same LLM is also used as the simulated target.

params.name: objective_scorer
params.desc: Scorer to evaluate the final turn.

params.name: num_turns
params.desc: Number of conversation turns to generate. Defaults to 3.

params.name: starting_sequence
params.desc:
The starting sequence number for the generated SeedPrompts.
Each message gets an incrementing sequence number. Defaults to 0.

params.name: adversarial_chat_system_prompt_path
params.desc: Path to the system prompt for the adversarial chat.

params.name: simulated_target_system_prompt_path
params.desc:
Path to the system prompt for the simulated target.
If None, no system prompt is used for the simulated target.

params.name: next_message_system_prompt_path
params.desc:
Optional path to a system prompt for generating
a final user message. If provided, after the simulated conversation, a single
LLM call generates a user message that attempts to get the target to fulfill
the objective in their next response. The prompt template receives `objective`
and `conversation_so_far` parameters.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Converter configuration for the attack. Defaults to None.

params.name: memory_labels
params.desc: Labels to associate with the conversation in memory. Defaults to None.

returns.desc: List of SeedPrompts representing the generated conversation, with sequence numbers
starting from `starting_sequence` and incrementing by 1 for each message.
User messages have role="user", assistant messages have role="assistant".
If next_message_system_prompt_path is provided, the last message will be a user message
generated to elicit the objective fulfillment.

raises.desc: If num_turns is not a positive integer.
